title: Fix dangling ~/dotfiles/git_template/info/exclude symlink
description:
This repo's .git/info/exclude was a symlink to ~/dotfiles/git_template/info/exclude, but that target file (and its parent dir) don't exist on this machine — a dangling symlink from the git template used by 'git init'/'git clone'.

Impact: git-nook (and git's own exclude handling) fail when appending to the exclude file through the dangling symlink. Worked around in the spry repo by replacing the symlink with a real .git/info/exclude file (local, unversioned), but this affects EVERY repo created from that template globally.

Fix: recreate ~/dotfiles/git_template/info/exclude (and its dir) in the dotfiles repo so the symlink resolves everywhere. This is a dotfiles-repo change, not a spry change.

Discovered 2026-07-11 while setting up the beads git-nook on spry.

title: sp land: stop retargeting PRs to trunk (per-PR diff fidelity)
description:
DESIGN / rationale for the sp land no-retarget fix. NOT yet implemented on main.

RELATED: the step-by-step implementation plan is bead spry-8lz (full verbatim plan doc inlined there).

SOURCE: this content summarizes the design doc that lived at docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md on the local branch fix/land-no-retarget (commit ff949c8). That branch also added an "Outstanding work" bullet to AGENTS.md pointing at the plan docs; both the branch and that AGENTS.md note are superseded by these two beads.

PROBLEM: landCommand retargets every in-scope PR base to config.trunk BEFORE the ff-push (src/commands/land.ts, the 'Retarget every scope PR to trunk BEFORE the push' loop, ~lines 147-161). This corrupts each merged PR's frozen three-dot diff. GitHub marks a PR MERGED on default-branch reachability (which the ff-push already guarantees), NOT on its base — so the retarget is unnecessary for MERGED status. But because it runs while trunk is still at old-trunk, each PR k's base becomes old-trunk and its frozen diff becomes old-trunk..head_k (cumulative: bottom PR shows 1 commit, top PR shows the whole stack + union of files).

FIX: Delete the retarget loop entirely; leave every PR on its stacked base through the push. Remove the retargetPR import from land.ts. KEEP retargetPR in src/gh/pr.ts / src/gh/index.ts — src/commands/sync.ts still uses it legitimately. Everything else unchanged (readiness gate, ff-push, cleanup tail: dropLandedFromPRCache/scrubLandedGroupRecords/deleteSpentBranches, autoDeleteOnLand). After: sp land makes zero 'gh pr edit' calls; only GitHub traffic is the readiness api graphql lookups.

OUT OF SCOPE (decided): no poll-until-MERGED; no delete_branch_on_merge / ruleset toggling; no change to autoDeleteOnLand. Accepted caveat: with autoDeleteOnLand on + 2+ PR stack, deleting a parent head branch before GitHub's async merge-marking can momentarily orphan a child PR (base vanishes -> may flip CLOSED). Accepted because autoDeleteOnLand is opt-in/default off; sp clean is the recovery tool.

TESTS (delete-and-replace in tests/commands/land.test.ts): DELETE test 'retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing' (~lines 264-322). ADD a replacement asserting a land over a stacked stack makes NO 'gh pr edit' calls while origin/main still advances to the target tip (count pr edit calls, expect zero, via stubGh). Other whole-stack/partial/readiness/cleanup tests are unaffected.

DOC + CASSETTE (must re-record — HUMAN-RUN): tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts fragment order 010 'Landing through a commit'. Rewrite the prose (~line 107) which currently falsely says retargeting is what makes PRs MERGED. New prose: sp land --through <id> lands from the bottom through <id>; fast-forwards trunk to that unit's tip; never uses the GitHub merge API and never retargets PR bases; GitHub marks each PR MERGED via default-branch reachability; leaving each PR on its stacked base keeps its diff scoped to that unit; sp land never deletes branches (sp clean's job). Requires SPRY_RECORD=1 cassette re-record (needs gh auth + HTTPS git) — ask the user to run it.

ALSO UPDATE: docs/rebuild-roadmap.md land decisions + CHANGELOG [Unreleased]. Full task breakdown is in spry-8lz.

title: Plan: sp land no-retarget fix — step-by-step implementation
description:
Step-by-step implementation plan for the sp land no-retarget fix. This is the verbatim content of the plan doc that lived at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget.md on the local branch fix/land-no-retarget (commit ff949c8), preserved here because beads is the only record. The design/rationale is the related bead spry-4k6. Task 5 (re-record land cassettes) is a HUMAN-RUN step: needs gh auth + HTTPS git config. NOT yet implemented on main.

# `sp land` — Stop Retargeting PRs to Trunk — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Remove `sp land`'s pre-push "retarget every PR to trunk" step so each merged PR keeps its own single-unit diff instead of a cumulative one.

**Architecture:** `sp land` currently retargets every in-scope PR's base to trunk _before_ the fast-forward push, which freezes a cumulative `old-trunk…head_k` diff into each merged PR. GitHub marks a PR `MERGED` on reachability from the **default branch**, not on its base — so the ff-push alone already yields `MERGED` status. We delete the retarget loop; land becomes a pure `git push` (plus readiness lookups and the existing cleanup tail). PRs keep their stacked bases, so each frozen diff stays scoped to its unit.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Bun (`bun test`), git plumbing, `gh` CLI via a cassette record/replay seam.

**Design doc:** [`docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md`](../../plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md)

**Environment note:** This machine has git 2.52.0 (≥ 2.40), so plain `bun test` / `bun run` are correct — no `:docker` alias needed. If executed on a machine with git < 2.40, substitute the `:docker` aliases per `AGENTS.md`.

---

## File Structure

| File                                                | Change            | Responsibility                                                                                    |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/commands/land.ts`                              | Modify            | Remove the retarget loop and the `retargetPR` import. Land becomes ff-push + readiness + cleanup. |
| `tests/commands/land.test.ts`                       | Modify            | Delete the retarget-ordering test; add a "makes no `gh pr edit` calls" test.                      |
| `tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts`                   | Modify            | Rewrite fragment-010 prose; remove the dead `retargeted PR #…` scrub.                             |
| `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json` | Re-record (human) | Faithful recording of the new no-retarget `gh` traffic.                                           |
| `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--020.json` | Re-record (human) | Same, via the interactive picker path.                                                            |
| `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}`            | Regenerate        | Reflect new prose; no "retargeted PR #…" line.                                                    |
| `docs/rebuild-roadmap.md`                           | Modify            | Correct the `sp land` decision text.                                                              |
| `CHANGELOG.md`                                      | Modify            | Add a `Fixed` entry; correct the two stale `Added` entries.                                       |

`retargetPR` **stays** in `src/gh/pr.ts` and `src/gh/index.ts` — `src/commands/sync.ts:384` still uses it. Only land's _use_ of it is removed.

---

## Task 1: Add the failing "no retarget" test (TDD red)

**Files:**

- Test: `tests/commands/land.test.ts` (add a test inside the existing `describe("sp land --through", ...)` block)

The existing harness (`stubGh`, `ghPrStub`, `publishedStack`, `runLand`, `captureLogs`, `trapExit`, `makeConfiguredRepo`, `makeCtx`) is already defined at the top of this file — reuse it. `stubGh` returns `{ gh, calls }`; each recorded call is `{ args: string[]; stdin?: string }`. A `gh pr edit` call has `args[0] === "pr" && args[1] === "edit"`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

Add this test immediately after the existing `"--through the first unit lands only the bottom…"` test (i.e. before the current `"retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing"` test, which Task 2 deletes):

```ts
  test("makes no `gh pr edit` calls: PRs keep their stacked bases, trunk still advances", async () => {
    const repo = await makeConfiguredRepo();
    const git = createRealGitRunner();
    await publishedStack(repo, git, [
      { id: "aaa11111", subject: "first" },
      { id: "bbb22222", subject: "second" },
    ]);
    const tip = (await git.run(["rev-parse", "HEAD"], { cwd: repo.path })).stdout.trim();

    // The upper PR (#2) is stacked on the lower unit's branch. Under the old
    // flow land would retarget it to `main` before pushing. It must not anymore.
    const { gh, calls } = stubGh(
      ghPrStub({
        "spry/test/aaa11111": { number: 1, base: "main" },
        "spry/test/bbb22222": { number: 2, base: "spry/test/aaa11111" },
      }),
    );
    const ctx = makeCtx(repo, gh);
    const logs = captureLogs();
    const trap = trapExit();
    try {
      await runLand(ctx, { cwd: repo.path, through: "bbb22222" });
    } finally {
      trap.restore();
      logs.restore();
    }

    // No PR was retargeted — land emits zero `gh pr edit` calls.
    const editCalls = calls.filter((c) => c.args[0] === "pr" && c.args[1] === "edit");
    expect(editCalls).toHaveLength(0);
    // MERGED-by-reachability still holds: origin/main advanced to the tip.
    const originMain = (
      await git.run(["rev-parse", "origin/main"], { cwd: repo.path })
    ).stdout.trim();
    expect(originMain).toBe(tip);
    expect(trap.exitCode).toBeUndefined();
  });
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test to verify it FAILS**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts -t "makes no .gh pr edit. calls"`

Expected: FAIL. `editCalls` will have length 1 (the current code retargets PR #2 to `main`), so `expect(editCalls).toHaveLength(0)` fails.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the red test**

```bash
git add tests/commands/land.test.ts
git commit -m "test(land): assert land makes no gh pr edit calls (red)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 2: Remove the retarget loop (TDD green)

**Files:**

- Modify: `src/commands/land.ts` (remove import of `retargetPR`; remove the retarget loop, current lines 147–161)

- [ ] **Step 1: Remove `retargetPR` from the import block**

In `src/commands/land.ts`, the import from `../gh/index.ts` currently reads:

```ts
import {
  findPRsForBranches,
  retargetPR,
  pushBranch,
  deleteRemoteBranch,
  isAlreadyGone,
} from "../gh/index.ts";
```

Change it to (drop the `retargetPR` line):

```ts
import {
  findPRsForBranches,
  pushBranch,
  deleteRemoteBranch,
  isAlreadyGone,
} from "../gh/index.ts";
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Delete the retarget loop**

Remove this entire block (currently step 5, between the unresolved-review-threads block and the ff-push `// 6. One ff push…`):

```ts
  // 5. Retarget every scope PR to trunk BEFORE the push (the secret sauce).
  for (const unit of scopeUnits) {
    const branch = branchForUnit(unit, config);
    const pr = prMap.get(branch);
    if (!pr || pr.state !== "OPEN") continue;
    if (pr.baseRefName === config.trunk) continue;
    try {
      await retargetPR(ctx, pr.number, config.trunk, { cwd });
      console.log(`↻ retargeted PR #${pr.number} → ${config.trunk}`);
    } catch (err) {
      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
      console.error(`✗ Could not retarget PR #${pr.number}: ${message}`);
      process.exit(1); // never ff with an un-retargeted middle PR
    }
  }

```

The comment on the next block currently says `// 6. One ff push to the target tip.` — renumber it to `// 5. One ff push to the target tip.` for tidiness (optional but keeps the numbered comments contiguous; the following cleanup comment `// 7.` → `// 6.` and `// 8.` → `// 7.` likewise). This is comment-only.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run the new test to verify it PASSES**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts -t "makes no .gh pr edit. calls"`

Expected: PASS. Land no longer calls `gh pr edit`; `origin/main` still advances to the tip via the ff-push.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify no unused-import / type errors**

Run: `bun run typecheck` (or the project's type-check script; check `package.json` `scripts` — if there is no dedicated script, `bunx tsc --noEmit` per the repo's tsconfig).

Expected: no error about an unused `retargetPR` import and no reference-to-undefined error (we removed both the import and its only use in this file together).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/commands/land.ts
git commit -m "fix(land): stop retargeting PRs to trunk before ff-push

Retargeting every in-scope PR to trunk before the fast-forward push froze
a cumulative old-trunk..head_k three-dot diff into each merged PR (bottom
PR shows 1 commit, top PR shows the whole stack). GitHub marks a PR MERGED
on reachability from the default branch, not on its base, so the ff-push
alone already yields MERGED status. Leave each PR on its stacked base so
its frozen diff stays scoped to its own unit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 3: Delete the obsolete retarget-ordering test

**Files:**

- Modify: `tests/commands/land.test.ts` (delete the `"retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing"` test)

This test's entire premise (retarget-before-push ordering) is the behavior we just removed. It builds a custom `ctx` that flags `mainAdvanced` on the ff-push and asserts `editsBeforePush` contains `"2"` — which can no longer happen.

- [ ] **Step 1: Delete the test**

In `tests/commands/land.test.ts`, delete the whole `test("retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing", async () => { … })` block (currently lines 264–322, from the `test(` line through its closing `});`). Leave the tests before and after it intact.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the land test file to verify the suite is green**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts`

Expected: PASS, with the retarget-ordering test gone and the new "makes no `gh pr edit` calls" test present. No test references the deleted block.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/commands/land.test.ts
git commit -m "test(land): drop obsolete retarget-before-push ordering test

Its premise (land retargets PRs to trunk before the ff-push) is the
behavior removed in the previous commit; superseded by the new
no-gh-pr-edit assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 4: Fix the doc-test prose and remove the dead scrub

**Files:**

- Modify: `tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts` (fragment order 010)

The doc test is the source of truth for `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}`. Its prose currently states the retarget-first mechanic, which is now false; and it scrubs a `retargeted PR #…` line that land no longer prints.

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite the fragment-010 prose**

In `tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts`, replace the `doc.prose(...)` call (currently line 106–108) with:

```ts
      doc.prose(
        "`sp land --through <id>` lands the stack from the bottom **through** the unit identified by `<id>` (a group ID, unit-ID prefix, or commit-hash prefix). Spry fast-forwards trunk to that unit's tip — it never uses the GitHub merge API and never retargets PR bases. GitHub marks each PR `MERGED` because its commits become reachable from the default branch; leaving each PR on its stacked base keeps that PR's diff scoped to just its own unit. `sp land` never deletes branches (that is `sp clean`'s job):",
      );
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Remove the dead scrub**

Delete this block (currently lines 110–112), including its explanatory comment:

```ts
      // PR numbers are GitHub-minted (non-deterministic); canonicalize the one
      // shown so the generated doc stays stable across re-recordings.
      doc.scrub(/retargeted PR #\d+/g, "retargeted PR #11");
```

Land no longer prints a "retargeted PR #N" line, so the scrub matches nothing. (The two `doc.scrub(...)` calls higher up — the repo scrub and the `spry-check` URL scrub — stay.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Update the code comment that mentions the removed retarget**

The comment inside the fragment (currently around line 90–93) reads:

```ts
      // Record mode publishes two real stacked PRs on spry-check and captures
      // land's full gh traffic (the embedded sync's retarget + land's own
      // retarget-to-trunk); replay serves it offline. Same body both ways — only
      // the git origin and the gh seam env differ.
```

Replace it with:

```ts
      // Record mode publishes two real stacked PRs on spry-check and captures
      // land's gh traffic (the embedded sync's stacked-base retarget + land's
      // readiness lookups); land itself no longer retargets to trunk. Replay
      // serves it offline. Same body both ways — only the git origin and the gh
      // seam env differ.
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run the doc test in REPLAY mode to confirm it still passes on the OLD cassette**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts -t "Landing through a commit"`

Expected: PASS. The replayer is args-keyed (`src/lib/replaying-client.ts`, `match: "args"`) and does not require every recorded entry to be consumed, so the now-unconsumed `pr edit … --base main` cassette entry is simply skipped. The generated fragment now carries the new prose and no "retargeted PR" line. (Task 5 re-records to remove the stale entry.)

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts docs/generated/commands/land.md docs/generated/commands/land.html
git commit -m "docs(land): correct land doc prose — no PR retargeting

Land no longer retargets PR bases to trunk; GitHub marks each PR MERGED via
default-branch reachability. Rewrite the fragment prose and drop the dead
'retargeted PR #…' scrub.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

> Note: if the doc-test run regenerates `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}` as a side effect, include them in this commit (shown above). If the generated docs are produced by a separate script rather than the test, run that script first and confirm the diff shows the new prose and no "retargeted PR" line before committing.

---

## Task 5: Re-record both land cassettes (HUMAN-RUN step)

**Files:**

- Re-record: `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json`
- Re-record: `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--020.json`
- Regenerate: `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}`

**This task cannot be run by the agent.** Recording needs `gh` auth, an HTTPS git config, and mutates the live `spry-check` repo. It is the validation step for the code+prose changes above (per `AGENTS.md`: "real-record mode is the validation"). Do it **after** Tasks 1–4 are committed so the recording captures the new no-retarget traffic.

Per `tests/fixtures/cassettes/README.md`, the "one hard rule" is never hand-author a cassette — every byte must come from a real `gh` invocation. That is exactly why we re-record instead of hand-editing out the stale `pr edit --base main` entry.

- [ ] **Step 1: Prepare the recording git config (once)**

Ensure `gh` is authenticated against an account owning a `spry-check` repo carrying the `<!-- spry-test-repo:v1 -->` safety marker (create it with `bun run scripts/setup-spry-check.ts` if needed). Create a minimal HTTPS git config so an SSH `insteadOf` rewrite can't hang the subprocess:

```
# /tmp/rec-gitconfig
[credential "https://github.com"]
	helper = !gh auth git-credential
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Re-record fragment 010**

```bash
GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/tmp/rec-gitconfig SPRY_RECORD=1 \
  bun test tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts -t "Landing through a commit"
```

Expected: rewrites `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json`; the fixture reset runs afterward. Verify the new cassette has **no** `["pr","edit",…,"--base","main"]` entry (land's retarget-to-trunk is gone). The `["pr","edit",…,"--base","spry/dondenton/aaa11111"]` entry (the embedded sync's stacked-base retarget) legitimately remains.

Sanity-check the entry (optional):

```bash
bun -e 'const c = await Bun.file("tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json").json(); console.log(c.entries.filter(e => e.args[0]==="pr" && e.args[1]==="edit").map(e => e.args.join(" ")))'
```

Expected output: exactly one `pr edit … --base spry/dondenton/aaa11111`, and no `--base main`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Re-record fragment 020**

```bash
GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/tmp/rec-gitconfig SPRY_RECORD=1 \
  bun test tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts -t "Picking the land point interactively"
```

Expected: rewrites `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--020.json`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Re-run both fragments in REPLAY mode against the fresh cassettes**

```bash
bun test tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts
```

Expected: PASS offline against the re-recorded cassettes. Confirm the regenerated `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}` show the new prose and no "retargeted PR #…" line.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit the re-recorded cassettes and regenerated docs**

```bash
git add tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json \
        tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--020.json \
        docs/generated/commands/land.md docs/generated/commands/land.html
git commit -m "test(land): re-record land cassettes without the retarget-to-trunk call

Recorded against spry-check on the no-retarget land flow, so the cassettes
contain no stale 'pr edit --base main' entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 6: Correct the roadmap decision text

**Files:**

- Modify: `docs/rebuild-roadmap.md`

The `sp land` decision bullet and the "What exists now" summary state that land retargets in-scope PRs to trunk. That is now reversed.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the "What exists now" `sp land` line**

Find the line under "What exists now" that begins `- \`sp land\` — retarget in-scope PRs to trunk and fast-forward trunk…`. Replace its opening clause so it no longer says land retargets. New text:

```markdown
- `sp land` — fast-forward trunk to the target unit's tip, landing every
  in-scope PR without touching PR bases; **`--through <id>`** lands from the
  bottom through a group/unit/commit id, bare `sp land` opens a single-select
  picker. After a successful ff-push it **scrubs the landed units' state** (drops
  their PR-cache entries and group records) and, when `spry.autoDeleteOnLand` is
  set, deletes their remote branches.
```

(Preserve the surrounding list formatting; only this bullet changes.)

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the `## Decisions` bullet for land**

Find the decision bullet beginning `- **\`sp land\` was redesigned, not ported.\*\*`. Its current text says "This branch instead retargets every in-scope PR to trunk and does a single fast-forward push of trunk to the target tip." Replace that sentence and add the reversal rationale so the bullet reads:

```markdown
- **`sp land` was redesigned, not ported.** `main`'s land merged PRs via the
  GitHub merge API (`--all` merged the stack bottom-up, waiting on each merge).
  This branch instead does a single fast-forward push of trunk to the target
  tip, marking every in-scope PR `MERGED` via reachability from the default
  branch. It never uses the merge API. `--all` is replaced by `--through <id>`
  (whole stack = through the top unit). "Behind trunk" surfaces as a
  fast-forward rejection pointing at `sp rebase`.
- **`sp land` does not retarget PR bases.** An earlier rebuild decision (see
  `docs/plans/2026-06-13-land-design.md`) retargeted every in-scope PR to trunk
  before the ff-push, on the theory that a PR is marked `MERGED` only when its
  head lands in its own base. That theory was wrong in a way that corrupted the
  record: GitHub marks a PR `MERGED` on reachability from the **default branch**,
  not its base, so the ff-push alone suffices — and retargeting while trunk was
  still at its old position froze a cumulative `old-trunk…head_k` three-dot diff
  into each merged PR (bottom PR = 1 commit, top PR = the whole stack). Leaving
  each PR on its stacked base keeps every PR's diff scoped to its own unit. See
  `docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md`.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add docs/rebuild-roadmap.md
git commit -m "docs(roadmap): record that sp land no longer retargets PR bases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 7: Changelog

**Files:**

- Modify: `CHANGELOG.md` (`[Unreleased]`)

Two existing `Added` entries under `[Unreleased]` describe the old retarget behavior and must be corrected, and a new `Fixed` entry records the behavior change.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add a `Fixed` entry**

At the top of the `### Fixed` list under `## [Unreleased]`, add:

```markdown
- `sp land` no longer retargets in-scope PRs to trunk before the fast-forward push. Retargeting while trunk was still at its old position froze a cumulative `old-trunk…head_k` three-dot diff into each merged PR — the bottom PR showed 1 commit, the top PR the entire stack and the union of every file it touched. GitHub marks a PR `MERGED` on reachability from the default branch (which the ff-push guarantees), not on its base, so the retarget was never needed for merged status. Land now leaves each PR on its stacked base, so every merged PR keeps its own single-unit diff. Land makes zero `gh pr edit` calls.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Correct the stale `Added` land entry**

Find the `Added` bullet beginning `- \`sp land\` — lands the stack into trunk by retargeting the in-scope PRs to trunk and fast-forwarding…`. Change the opening so it no longer claims retargeting. Replace the sentence:

> "It runs a full `sp sync` first, gates on live PR readiness (…), retargets every scope PR to trunk before pushing, and surfaces "behind trunk" as a fast-forward rejection pointing at `sp rebase`."

with:

> "It runs a full `sp sync` first, gates on live PR readiness (…), fast-forwards trunk to the target tip without retargeting PR bases, and surfaces "behind trunk" as a fast-forward rejection pointing at `sp rebase`."

Also change the entry's opening clause `— lands the stack into trunk by retargeting the in-scope PRs to trunk and fast-forwarding` → `— lands the stack into trunk by fast-forwarding`. (Keep the "(…)" readiness detail verbatim from the existing entry — only the retarget clauses change.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Correct the stale cassette `Added` entry**

It ends with a clause: "…and both `sp land` doc fragments (`--through`and the interactive picker) now record/replay real`gh`traffic the same way — including land's full flow (the embedded`sp sync` retarget plus land's own retarget-to-trunk)." with:

> "— including land's full flow (the embedded `sp sync` stacked-base retarget; land itself does not retarget PR bases)."

git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "docs(changelog): record sp land no-retarget fix; correct stale entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

title: sp sync: reorder retarget marks PRs merged (two-phase park-on-trunk fix)
description:
PROBLEM: When commits in a stack are REORDERED and then `sp sync`ed, the stacked-PR bases are re-adjusted in a single in-order pass (retargetMismatched in src/commands/sync.ts ~lines 365-393, driven by expectedBaseFor at ~line 355). Because the target/base branches are not readjusted in the right order relative to the push, PRs can transiently end up with their head reachable from the default (trunk) branch, and GitHub then marks them MERGED (GitHub marks a PR MERGED on reachability from the default branch, not on its base). Net effect after a reorder-sync: all the PRs in the stack show as merged.

WHY IT HAPPENS: retargetMismatched walks `units` in order and, for each PR whose baseRefName != expectedBaseFor(unit), calls retargetPR to the new expected base. After a reorder, the expected bases form a different chain than what is currently on GitHub. Retargeting one PR at a time, interleaved with (or after) a push that has already moved branch tips, means intermediate base/head combinations can make a PR's commits look reachable from trunk. There is no ordering discipline that guarantees no PR is ever transiently based such that it appears merged.

PROPOSED FIX (escapable via a two-phase approach): 
1. DETECT reorder: before retargeting, recognize whether the PR bases have been reordered (current on-GitHub base chain differs from the newly-expected base chain from expectedBaseFor over the stack order).
2. PARK: if reordered, first retarget ALL affected PRs' bases to the trunk/main branch.
3. PUSH: push the reordered branch tips.
4. FINAL RETARGET: then retarget each PR to its correct new stacked base (expectedBaseFor), in order.

Parking everyone on trunk first breaks the transient reachability chains so no PR is momentarily reachable-from-trunk-via-its-base during the shuffle, then the final retarget rebuilds the correct stacked chain.

RELATED: this is adjacent to spry-4k6 / spry-8lz (sp LAND no-retarget fix), but distinct — that work removes retargeting from `sp land`; this issue is about `sp sync`'s retarget ORDER when commits are reordered. The land fix leaves retargetPR in place precisely because sync still needs it (src/commands/sync.ts:34, :384).

FILES: src/commands/sync.ts — retargetMismatched (~365-393), expectedBaseFor (~355-363), the push+retarget sequencing around lines 144-167 and 515-555. Tests: tests/commands/sync.test.ts (+ sync.doc.test.ts / cassette re-record if gh traffic changes).

NEEDS: confirm the exact reachability/merge-marking mechanism with a reproduction (reorder a 2-3 PR stack, sync, observe all PRs flip MERGED) before implementing; design the reorder-detection predicate; decide whether park-on-trunk is unconditional on any reorder or only when a specific unsafe transition is detected.

title: EPIC: sp land no-retarget fix
description:
EPIC: sp land no-retarget fix. Design: spry-4k6. Verbatim plan: spry-8lz. Executed via superpowers subagent-driven-development.

STATUS 2026-07-12: MERGED TO MAIN (merge commit b15ef1b, pushed). The core fix shipped: sp land no longer retargets in-scope PRs to trunk; it fast-forwards trunk to the target tip and leaves each PR on its stacked base, so each merged PR keeps its own single-unit diff (GitHub marks MERGED via default-branch reachability). Land makes zero gh pr edit calls.

DONE: Task 1 (red test, 1d85a28), Task 2 (remove loop, 9c0ab40), Task 3 (drop obsolete test, dff9e1f), Task 4 (doc prose + regen, 0e1aa21), Task 6 (roadmap) + Task 7 (changelog) (8323cff), Task 8 (full suite green 558/0, merged). Each code task spec+quality reviewed via subagents.

REMAINING: Task 5 (re-record land cassettes) is OPEN and blocked by pre-existing bug spry-xil (sp land embedded sync computes 1-commit stack in record mode; reproduces on clean main). The shipped change is fully verified by the offline REPLAY suite; only real-gh cassette refresh is deferred. Also filed spry-bei (baseline pollution) in passing.

CLOSE THIS EPIC once spry-xil is fixed and Task 5 cassettes are re-recorded. Left open intentionally per that remaining work.

Test builds a 2-PR stack (aaa11111 base main, bbb22222 base spry/test/aaa11111), runs land through bbb22222, asserts zero 'pr edit' calls AND origin/main == tip. Must FAIL first (editCalls length 1 under current code). Verbatim test body in spry-8lz Task 1. Commit red test.

Retarget every scope PR to trunk BEFORE the push' block). (3) Renumber the following comments 6->5, 7->6, 8->7 (comment-only). Verify new test passes and 'bun run types' is clean (no unused-import error). KEEP retargetPR in src/gh/pr.ts and src/gh/index.ts — sync.ts still uses it. Commit fix.

Its premise (retarget-before-push ordering) is the removed behavior. Run 'bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts' green. Commit.

(2) Delete the dead doc.scrub(/retargeted PR #.../g, ...) at line 112 and its 2-line comment. (3) Update the 'Record mode publishes...' code comment (~line 90) to the new wording. Then merge fix/land-no-retarget back to main and confirm tests pass on main. This gates closing the epic spry-dzp.

title: Discovery: land cassette recording pollutes spry-check main; fixture reset does not restore baseline
description: While re-recording the sp land cassettes (spry-dzp.5 / spry-8lz Task 5), the record run failed with 'No commit or group matching bbb22222 found in stack' and the embedded sp sync only pushed one branch. ROOT CAUSE: spry-check's main was left pointing at a landed commit 'Add login / Spry-Commit-Id: aaa11111' from a PRIOR land recording. sp land's whole job is to fast-forward main to a stack tip, so recording it MUTATES spry-check's main. createGitHubFixture().reset() (used by the doc test) evidently does NOT roll main back to the pristine single-commit baseline that scripts/setup-spry-check.ts establishes. On the next record run, because main already contains aaa11111, the stack parse no longer sees a clean 2-commit stack and land aborts. WORKAROUND used this session: re-run 'SPRY_TEST_REPO_SETUP_SKIP_CONFIRM=1 bun run scripts/setup-spry-check.ts' to reset main to one commit before each land re-record. FIX TO CONSIDER: make the land doc-test fixture reset restore main to the baseline commit (or have the land fragments record against a throwaway ref / reset main in setup), so land cassettes are re-recordable without a manual setup-script run. Files: tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts (setupLandStack + fixture.reset), tests/lib/ github fixture (createGitHubFixture), scripts/setup-spry-check.ts. Relates to epic spry-dzp.

title: sp land record-mode failure: stale refs/spry/groups on spry-check collapses stack into 1 group unit
description:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (issue's original premise was WRONG). Verified via instrumented real record run (SPRY_RECORD=1, SPRY_DEBUG_STACK hook in getStackCommits + setup dump in land.doc.test.ts).

The stack is NOT computed as 1 commit. getStackCommits correctly returns 2 commits every time: 'e9dcb2f:Add login | a9520ad:Add logout', base=be372b3 (clean origin/main), HEAD=a9520ad. The bug is one layer UP, in GROUPING.

MECHANISM: spry-check has a STALE custom ref left over from prior record runs:
  refs/spry/groups -> blob keyed 'aaa11111' = {"title":"","members":["aaa11111","bbb22222"]}
(Also stale on the remote: refs/spry/prs with a cached PR #1040, and an ancient pre-rebuild refs/spry/happycollision/stack-settings.)

Because the test commit-ids are DETERMINISTIC (aaa11111/bbb22222 reproduced every run), this months-old group record still matches today's commits. Failure chain:
  1. Setup builds feature/x = [baseline, aaa11111, bbb22222] -- correct, 2 commits, origin/main clean at baseline.
  2. sp land -> embedded sp sync -> fetchGroupRecords pulls stale refs/spry/groups from spry-check.
  3. buildCommitGroupMap maps BOTH aaa11111->group aaa11111 AND bbb22222->group aaa11111.
  4. getStackCommits returns 2 commits, but detectPRUnits (src/parse/stack.ts:16) collapses them into ONE group unit id=aaa11111.
  5. Sync pushes only spry/dondenton/aaa11111, caches 1 PR, prints 'Updated PR cache (1 PR)' + 'Sync complete'.
  6. Land re-parses -> 1 unit -> resolveUpTo('bbb22222') fails -> 'Error: No commit or group matching bbb22222 found in stack' -> exit 1.
Replay PASSES because the local bare origin has no refs/spry/*; only real spry-check carries the stale ref.

WHY IT PERSISTS: tests/lib/github-fixture.ts reset() only closeAllPRs() + deleteAllBranches() (refs/heads/*). It NEVER deletes refs/spry/* custom refs. So a group record pushed by any prior land/sync/group record run sticks forever.

FIX (two parts):
  (a) Immediate unblock: delete the 3 stale refs from spry-check:
      git push origin --delete refs/spry/groups refs/spry/prs refs/spry/happycollision/stack-settings
  (b) Durable: extend github-fixture.ts reset() to also purge refs/spry/* (e.g. gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs then DELETE each ref under refs/spry/, or a git push --delete of the discovered spry refs). Add to CleanupReport. This makes the deterministic-commit-id design safe again for record mode.

NOTE: this is unrelated to spry-dzp (no-retarget) -- confirmed pre-existing, as the issue already said. It blocks re-recording land cassettes (spry-dzp.5).

Files: tests/lib/github-fixture.ts (reset -- THE fix), src/parse/stack.ts:16 detectPRUnits (correct behavior, not a bug), src/commands/sync.ts:85 fetchGroupRecords, src/git/group-titles.ts fetchGroupRecords. Relates to spry-bei.
close_reason:
FIXED (both parts).

(a) Immediate unblock — DONE: deleted the 3 stale refs from live spry-check via gh api DELETE:
    refs/spry/groups, refs/spry/happycollision/stack-settings, refs/spry/prs.
    Verified matching-refs/spry/ now returns empty. Record-mode blocker cleared.

(b) Durable fix — DONE (commit f7db774 on branch fix/purge-spry-refs-on-reset):
    Extended tests/lib/github-fixture.ts reset() with a new purgeSpryRefs() step that
    lists refs via `gh api .../git/matching-refs/spry/` and DELETEs each. Added
    CleanupReport.spryRefsDeleted count + a skipIf(SPRY_RECORD) test that seeds a
    stale ref and asserts reset() purges it. Changelog updated.

Validation:
  - Full offline suite: 558 pass, 0 fail (unchanged by the CleanupReport shape change).
  - bun run check: 0 errors, format clean.
  - purgeSpryRefs API shape live-validated by hand doing part (a): matching-refs/spry/
    listed exactly the 3 refs, and DELETE .../git/refs/{refs-stripped-path} removed all
    3 (incl. the nested happycollision/stack-settings) — the exact calls the code makes.

CAVEAT: the new SPRY_RECORD-gated test's own red->green cycle was NOT executed (it needs
gh auth + network like its siblings and skips offline). Its logic mirrors the live-validated
calls above. It runs for real on the next record run (spry-dzp.5).

title: Discovery: gh api -X DELETE returns exit 0 on 422; purgeSpryRefs can silently over-count under GitHub eventual consistency
description:
Found while verifying spry-xil fix by actually running the new SPRY_RECORD test.

SYMPTOM: tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts 'reset purges all refs/spry/*' intermittently fails: after fixture.reset(), a seeded refs/spry/test-purge ref survives even though purgeSpryRefs() reported deleting 1.

ROOT CAUSE: two compounding facts.
  1. GitHub git-refs API is eventually consistent. Immediately POSTing refs/spry/test-purge then DELETE/list on a different replica can 422 ('Reference does not exist') because the create hasn't propagated. Measured intermittently (~1 in 10-20 immediate seed->delete cycles 422s; most runs are clean 10/10).
  2. `gh api -X DELETE .../git/refs/spry/test-purge` returns EXIT 0 even when the HTTP status is 422 (verified with -i: 'HTTP/2.0 422 Unprocessable Entity', shell exit 0). So purgeSpryRefs()'s `if (deleteResult.exitCode === 0) deleted++` counts a no-op delete as success -> spryRefsDeleted over-counts AND the ref survives.

WHY IT MATTERS: purgeSpryRefs exists to guarantee stale refs/spry/* are gone before a record run proceeds (the whole point of spry-xil). A silent over-count that leaves a ref behind reintroduces the exact stale-group-record bug spry-xil fixed.

FIX DIRECTION (code, not just test): make purgeSpryRefs verify each delete actually removed the ref rather than trusting gh's exit code. Options: DELETE with -i and require HTTP 204; or re-list after delete and retry with small backoff until the ref is gone (handles eventual-consistency lag). Also make the test tolerant of the create->settle window (poll after seed before reset). 

FILES: tests/lib/github-fixture.ts purgeSpryRefs(); tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts. Discovered under commit f7db774 (branch fix/purge-spry-refs-on-reset). Relates to spry-xil.

title: Discovery: SPRY_RECORD network tests in github-fixture.test.ts flake on the 5s default timeout
description:
The pre-existing network tests in tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts ('createGitHubFixture resolves...', 'verifyTestRepo passes...', 'reset leaves zero open PRs') have NO explicit per-test timeout, so they use bun's 5000ms default. Each does 1-3 real `gh api` round-trips against live spry-check; under load a single call can exceed 5s and the test times out (observed 'verifyTestRepo passes against the real spry-check repo' timing out at 5000.99ms in a full-file run, then passing when run alone).

These only run under SPRY_RECORD (skipped offline), so they don't affect the default suite, but they make record runs flaky. FIX: give each an explicit generous timeout (e.g. 30000-60000ms), matching the new 'reset purges all refs/spry/*' test (60000). FILE: tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts. Discovered while verifying spry-xil. Relates to spry-xil, spry-oma.
